description:
You are assisting a researcher who is trying to get a concise,
    machine–readable overview of three experimental datasets that already exist
    on the system.

    1. Location of data  
       • All datasets are located inside the directory  
         /home/user/datasets/  

       • Inside that directory there are exactly three sub-directories, one per
         experiment:  
           /home/user/datasets/expA  
           /home/user/datasets/expB  
           /home/user/datasets/expC  

       • Each of those sub-directories contains only regular files (no further
         nested directories and no symlinks).

    2. What the researcher needs  
       Create a text-file report named  
         /home/user/dataset_summary.log  
       that lists, for every experiment directory, the following three pieces
       of information:

         a) the directory name (expA, expB, expC – *without* the full path)  
         b) the total size of all regular files in that directory, measured in **bytes**  
         c) the count of regular files in that directory  

    3. Output format that *must* be followed  
       • The file /home/user/dataset_summary.log must contain **exactly four
         lines**.  
       • Use plain ASCII text, one line per record, no leading/trailing
         blanks.  
       • Fields on each line are comma-separated, with **no spaces** between
         them.  
       • Lines must appear in descending order of total size (the directory
         with the largest total byte size first).  
       • After the three experiment lines, append a fourth line that
         consolidates the grand totals and starts with the literal word
         “TOTAL”.  
       • Formal grammar for each line:  
         <name>,<total_bytes>,<file_count>

       Example *structure* (values shown here are placeholders – your script
       must calculate the real values):  
         expB,6656,3  
         expA,3072,3  
         expC,1555,2  
         TOTAL,11283,8

    4. Restrictions  
       • Accomplish the task with a **simple sequence of three to four standard
         shell commands** (e.g. combinations of “du”, “find”, “wc”, “sort”, etc.).  
       • Do **not** hard-code the byte totals or file counts; derive them from
         the actual file system contents.  
       • Do everything as the normal “user” account; no sudo is available.  
       • Overwrite /home/user/dataset_summary.log if it already exists.

    5. When you are done  
       • The only deliverable is the correctly formatted
         /home/user/dataset_summary.log file.  
       • No other files should be created or modified.
truth:
Initial data before the agent starts:

    1. Directory tree
       /home/user/datasets/
       ├── expA
       │   ├── data1.txt   (1024 bytes)
       │   ├── data2.txt   ( 512 bytes)
       │   └── notes.log   (1536 bytes)
       ├── expB
       │   ├── image1.jpg  (2048 bytes)
       │   ├── image2.jpg  (4096 bytes)
       │   └── readme.md   ( 512 bytes)
       └── expC
           ├── sample.dat  (1234 bytes)
           └── more.dat    ( 321 bytes)

    2. Correct byte and file totals
       expA  : 3 files,  3072 bytes
       expB  : 3 files,  6656 bytes
       expC  : 2 files,  1555 bytes
       TOTAL : 8 files, 11283 bytes

    3. Reference content expected in /home/user/dataset_summary.log
       (newline = LF):
         expB,6656,3\n
         expA,3072,3\n
         expC,1555,2\n
         TOTAL,11283,8\n

    4. Permissions
       • /home/user/datasets            : 755
       • all sub-directories            : 755
       • all files inside those dirs    : 644
       • /home/user/dataset_summary.log : must end up as 644 (agent may create it with default umask)
